description: Implement admin authentication and admin pages for creating albums and uploading photos.
steps: Created src/middleware.ts for admin route protection
Created src/app/admin/albums/[id]/page.tsx for album editing and photo management
Created API routes for album CRUD and photo operations
acceptance_criteria: Admin login page at /admin/login with hardcoded credentials from .env
Middleware protects /admin routes and redirects to login if not authenticated
Admin dashboard shows all albums with photo count and cover thumbnails
Create new album form with name and description
Edit album page with photo upload to Supabase Storage
Set cover photo and delete photos functionality
All API routes handle errors gracefully
Dark theme consistent with public pages
findings: 1. Next.js 16 shows a deprecation warning for middleware.ts file convention (suggests using 'proxy' instead), but it still works correctly. 2. Dynamic route params in Next.js 16 API routes use Promise-based params: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> } requiring await. 3. Admin pages are all client components using 'use client' since they depend on useState/useEffect. 4. File uploads use FormData with request.formData() in API routes. 5. Supabase Storage upload uses supabase.storage.from('photos').upload(path, file) with contentType option.
